idea-plugin.name: Claude Watch
idea-plugin.description:
A Cursor-like "the agent changed this file" experience for IntelliJ. Watches your project on disk and detects changes made by an external process — Claude Code, other AI agents, CLIs, or git — even while IntelliJ is in the background. It then opens the changed file, jumps to the first changed line, highlights the change with a fading marker, offers a diff against the pre-change content, groups bursts of edits, and logs a timeline. Includes a tool window, status-bar widget, pause mode, and settings.

Why it works in the background: a built-in refresh driver syncs the Virtual File System with disk on an interval, so external edits are seen without needing to focus the IDE window (and on non-boot volumes where the native file watcher is unreliable).

Disclaimer: not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Anthropic. "Claude" is a trademark of Anthropic, PBC. This is an independent, community-built tool that reacts to external file changes from any source.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.2 — Removed deprecated ReadAction.compute usages (clean against 2026.1); no behavior change.
0.1.1 — First public release: external-change detection that works while the IDE is backgrounded, open + jump + fading highlight, Show Diff, burst grouping, timeline tool window, status-bar widget, pause mode, settings.
